summary: This endpoint is a live-verified geography aggregation surface for disaster-designated spending. It rolls DEFC-filtered awards into state, county, or district totals and can switch between recipient geography and place of performance geography.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint to answer where disaster-designated federal spending was directed geographically for a chosen DEFC set and spending measure, especially for maps, ranking lists, and per-capita comparisons.
analyticalGrain: One row per returned geography shape_code at the requested geo_layer and scope, with a live-observed uncoded aggregate bucket when geography assignment is missing and geo_layer_filters are omitted.

primaryEntities.name: disaster geography aggregate
primaryEntities.description: A rolled-up spending record for one geography code at the selected state, county, or district layer after applying disaster filters.

primaryEntities.name: DEFC-filtered award population
primaryEntities.description: The set of awards included in the geography rollup, defined primarily by filter.def_codes and optionally narrowed by award type or shared search filters.

measures.name: amount
measures.description: Summed dollars for the requested spending_type, such as obligation, outlay, or face value of loan.

measures.name: award_count
measures.description: Number of awards contributing to each geography total.

measures.name: per_capita
measures.description: Per-capita geography metric derived from amount and population when population is available.

dimensions.name: geography layer
dimensions.description: Choose state, county, or district to set the response grain and the interpretation of shape_code.

dimensions.name: geographic scope
dimensions.description: Switch between recipient location and place of performance geography before aggregating the spending.

dimensions.name: DEFC code
dimensions.description: Disaster Emergency Fund Codes determine which disaster-designated spending population is included.

dimensions.name: award type
dimensions.description: Optional award_type_codes can narrow the rollup to contracts, grants, loans, and related award categories.

dimensions.name: time period and recipient filters
dimensions.description: The current raw profile indicates that shared search-filter keys such as time_period, recipient_scope, and recipient_locations can further narrow the aggregated award population.

suitableQuestions.name: Which states received the most disaster obligations for a DEFC?
suitableQuestions.description: Use geo_layer=state and spending_type=obligation to rank state totals for one or more DEFC codes.

suitableQuestions.name: How do recipient-location and place-of-performance geographies differ?
suitableQuestions.description: Compare the same DEFC and spending_type across different scope values to see where money was received versus where work was performed.

suitableQuestions.name: Which geographies have the highest disaster spending per capita?
suitableQuestions.description: Use the returned per_capita metric to compare geography burden or concentration where population is available.

notSuitableFor.name: Award-level or transaction-level detail
notSuitableFor.description: The endpoint returns aggregates by geography and does not include award identifiers, recipient names, or transaction records.

notSuitableFor.name: General federal spending outside disaster-designated funds
notSuitableFor.description: The request is anchored on DEFC filtering, so it is not the right surface for all-spending geographic analysis.

notSuitableFor.name: Trend analysis over time in a single response
notSuitableFor.description: Although shared time filters may narrow the population, the response grain is geography only and has no time bucket dimension.

joins.to: reference geography tables or external map shapes
joins.description: Map or enrich the returned geography rows by joining shape_code to the appropriate geography reference for the selected layer.

workflows.name: Map first, drill down second
workflows.description: Use this endpoint to identify high-spending geographies, then reuse the same DEFC filter with non-null state shape_code values translated into downstream award-search location filters.

steps.action: Call this endpoint with filter.def_codes, geo_layer, scope, and spending_type to identify notable geography rows.

For state-level follow-up, keep the same filter.def_codes and map each non-null state shape_code into a downstream award-search location object such as {"country":"USA","state":"MD"}. When this endpoint used recipient_location scope or its default, place those objects under recipient_locations with recipient_scope=domestic; when this endpoint used place_of_performance scope, place them under place_of_performance_locations with place_of_performance_scope=domestic.

Insert that filter block into the downstream award-search request template (for example POST /api/v2/search/spending_by_award/) and do not translate the uncoded null shape_code bucket into a state filter because it is not a usable state code.

caveats.statement: The staged markdown internally disagrees about whether the POST body is a string or an object; live probes confirm you must send the object form shown in the example body.
The response can include an uncoded aggregate bucket with null shape_code and display_name when geography assignment is missing and geo_layer_filters are omitted.
DEFC validation is case-sensitive and not uniform across bad inputs: lowercase values returned 400 while an empty array returned 422.
County and district layers, face_value_of_loan, and shared filter passthrough fields remain documented or inferred but were not reverified in this run.
